title: "The Seven Years Of Dearth Began" Genesis 41:53-57

notes.introduction: An exposition of Genesis 41:53-57 on the onset of the seven-year famine in Egypt, tracing the cry of the famished people to Pharaoh, Pharaoh's direction to Joseph, and the typological significance of Joseph as a figure of Christ and Egypt as a figure of the world system.
themes: egypt as a type of the world

sections.heading: Review of Genesis 41 and Seven Years of Plenty
The Seven Years of Dearth Begin
The Cry of the People to Pharaoh

Isaiah 30 and 31: Indictment Against Seeking Egypt
John 2:5 and Whatsoever He Saith, Do It
